mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.4pp, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that sustains corporate earnings and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply chain bottlenecks and reduced factory output, causing short-term equity sell-offs as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth does not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the signal loses predictive power during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the core consumer price index exceeds 3.5 percent annualized growth, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening that raises discount rates and forces negative equity valuation adjustments.
failure_condition: Equity markets absorb CPIAUCSL readings above 3.5% without negative 20-day returns, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls alongside rising unemployment rates indicates labor market fragmentation that weakens consumer confidence and drives negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or joint modeling yields no incremental explanatory power over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly translates to stronger corporate revenue fundamentals, generating immediate positive market repricing over the following trading week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with 5-day forward S&P 500 returns, or the signal produces negative alpha after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production combined with falling unemployment signals an overheating economy where capacity constraints force central banks to hike rates, triggering negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined INDPRO and UNRATE threshold fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or positive returns dominate during late-cycle overheating phases.
